channel.title: ASO Daily
channel.description: Daily briefing on agentic search optimization (GEO/AEO/AI search) — curated by Veronica Romney for the Marketing Suite GM Org.

item.title: ASO Daily — Wednesday, May 20, 2026
item.description: GEO measurement gap quantified: 43% of marketers are running GEO programs in 2026, but only 14% are tracking AI citation visibility. Also: AI Mode drives 93% zero-click while brands cited in AI Overviews earn 35% more organic and 91% more paid clicks.
item.content:encoded:
ASO Daily

Your morning briefing on agentic search optimization
Wednesday, May 20, 2026Vol. 1 · Issue No. 3Curated for Veronica Romney
Today’s Lead

The GEO measurement gap is now quantified: 43% running programs, 14% measuring them

Nearly half of all marketers name GEO a core 2026 strategy, yet barely one in seven track AI citation visibility. That’s not an execution gap — it’s a governance vacuum. The platform that cracks measurement-as-a-service owns the next two years of enterprise renewals.

Top Stories

37% of consumers start product searches on AI — not Google. ChatGPT holds 60.7% of AI search market share. A brand invisible on ChatGPT is invisible to a third of the category’s consideration-stage buyers. (Search Engine Land)

AI Overviews cited brands earn 35% more organic clicks — and 91% more paid clicks. AI Mode drives 93% zero-click behavior, but brands that earn citations convert at dramatically higher rates. Brands with consistent citation AND mention are 40% more likely to resurface across consecutive queries.

Industry Pulse

[Research] Princeton confirms: content structured with citations, statistics, and quotations improves AI visibility 30–40% vs. unoptimized content.

[Product] Best-of listicles = 43.8% of ChatGPT cited page types. Pages with 3+ schema types carry 13% higher citation likelihood.

[Funding] The tracked operator cohort has collectively raised $200M+: Profound $155M ($1B valuation, 10% of Fortune 500), Bluefish $68M, Evertune $19M, Scrunch $15M, AthenaHQ $2.7M (YC).

LinkedIn unavailable this run — Chrome browser not connected. Operator/Voice sections reflect research context only.

item.title: ASO Daily — Tuesday, May 19, 2026
item.description: A Wiley-published GEO book (Ross Hudgens, Siege Media) signals mainstream category legitimacy — Lily Ray amplified 16h ago. Zero-click rates hit 83-93% with AI Overviews; Scrunch closes $15M Series A; Jason Barnard introduces the "delegation boundary" framework for brand AI confidence.
item.content:encoded:
ASO Daily

Your morning briefing on agentic search optimization

Tuesday, May 19, 2026  ·  Vol. 1, Issue 2  ·  Curated for Veronica Romney
TODAY'S LEAD

A mainstream publisher just greenlit a book called "GEO" — the category now has an ISBN

Ross Hudgens (CEO, Siege Media) is writing GEO for Wiley, Q4 2026. Lily Ray amplified 16h ago. When a major trade publisher assigns a book to a category, that category has crossed from practitioner niche to institutional legitimacy.

Top Stories

37% of consumers now start searches on AI, not Google — Search Engine Land behavioral study. ChatGPT at 60.7% AI search share.

Zero-click rate hits 83–93% with AI Overviews / AI Mode. GEO delivers 4.4x higher conversions vs. traditional SEO at $3.71 ROI per dollar.

Operator Signals

James Cadwallader (Profound): Shared Ad Age Tech Power List — positioning Profound in mainstream ad-tech trade press alongside Bose and Reddit.

All other tracked operators quiet overnight.

Voice Highlights

Lily Ray (16h): Reposted Hudgens' GEO book video. Also: YouTube appearance "Lily Ray on AI Slop, GEO, and What Actually Works."

Jason Barnard (5d): "Delegation boundary" — AI pre-screens brands before users see results. Competing for AI confidence, not clicks. Cross-posted to Search Engine Land.

Industry Pulse

[Funding] Scrunch $15M Series A · 500+ enterprise brands including Lenovo.

[Research] 94% of digital leaders increasing AEO/GEO spend in 2026 — Conductor CMO Report.

[Recognition] Ad Age Tech Power List coverage signals GEO's arrival in mainstream ad trade media.

item.title: ASO Daily — Monday, May 18, 2026
item.description: Citation Outcome Engineering emerges as the new operating frame for GEO. The category is shifting from generic content activity to verified answer inclusion. Plus: Bluefish closes $43M Series B, Brandlight named CB Insights GEO market leader, HireClix launches JobFlow AEO.
item.content:encoded:
Today's Lead: Citation Outcome Engineering emerges as the new operating frame for GEO

The category is shifting from generic content activity to verified answer inclusion. With 50% of content cited in AI answers being less than 13 weeks old, recency is now a competitive moat — and operators measuring citation outcomes (not impressions) are pulling ahead.

Top Stories

The Death of the Blue Link (National Law Review)
Enterprise legal and compliance teams are flagging GEO as a material disclosure topic. With ChatGPT serving 800M+ weekly users, boards are asking whether brands have a documented strategy for being cited inside AI answers.

60% of U.S. consumers now use generative AI for product research (eMarketer)
Generative AI overtook traditional search for product discovery in three categories: travel, consumer electronics, and SaaS.

Industry Pulse

  • Funding: Bluefish closes $43M Series B. ~10% of the Fortune 500 (Adidas, AmEx, LVMH, Ulta) using it to manage AI visibility.
  • Recognition: Brandlight named GEO market leader by CB Insights, joining Bluefish on the AI 100 list.
  • Product: HireClix launches JobFlow AEO — first vertical AEO play, optimizing job postings to appear in ChatGPT and Perplexity answers.

ASO Daily · Vol. 1 · Issue No. 1 · Curated for Marketing Suite GM Org
This is the inaugural issue. Tomorrow's edition will include overnight LinkedIn posts from the 33 founders and thought leaders Veronica follows.
